items.title: Early Farrer voters make choices known as pre-polling gets underway in blockbuster by-election
items.summary: Pre-polling commences in the seat of Farrer for the hotly contested by-election triggered by the resignation of former opposition leader Sussan Ley.

items.title: El Niño warming could be unprecedented, but don't assume record drought in Australia
items.summary: Australia's weather pattern is in a reset, and the weather events happening this month will act like the ingredients to determine the flavour of our climate for the rest of the year.

items.title: What we know about the night 5yo girl Sharon Granites went missing in Alice Springs
items.summary: Five-year-old Sharon Granites vanished from an Alice Springs town camp late on Saturday night, with police alleging she has been abducted. Here's what we know about the night she went missing.

items.title: Western NSW dam limit increase plan dumped over impact on protected wetlands
items.summary: Water users in part of the Murray-Darling Basin are anxious that a proposal to protect their interests during droughts has been abandoned, as the dry conditions continue to worsen.

items.title: Parents slam 'weak' response at The Friends' School after girls targeted in AI deepfake pornography scandal
items.summary: Two parents of girls at a private school have criticised the institution's response to a deepfake incident, saying they were encouraged not to tell their daughters they had been identified in material shared in a boys' group chat.

items.title: SEQ first-home buyers turn to Melbourne as 5% Deposit Scheme provides limited opportunity
items.summary: South-East Queensland has been under-represented in 5% Deposit Scheme approvals, with prospective first-home buyers heading to Melbourne for its "considerable affordability" benefits.

items.title: Former ASIO chief questioned scrutiny of Bondi gunmen before quitting royal commission
items.summary: While advising the royal commission examining the Bondi Beach terrorist attack, the former head of Australia's domestic intelligence agency questioned whether early assessments of gunman Naveed Akram were sufficiently rigorous.

items.title: Queenslanders urged to get tested for viral hepatitis after liver cancer deaths triple since 1980s
items.summary: An alarming new report has found liver cancer deaths have risen from 1.6 deaths per 100,000 people in the 1980s to 5.1 per 100,000 in 2018–2022.

items.title: Fuel security a 'tricky balancing act', experts say as budget nears
items.summary: As the international fuel crisis continues to cause concern, the federal government is under pressure to provide a fix during next month's budget.

items.title: Amount of antibiotic florfenicol used at Tasmanian salmon farm lease concerning, say Greens, expert
items.summary: Almost 3 tonnes of the antibiotic florfenicol were used at one Tasmanian salmon farm lease in just two months, with information on its use across eight other sites yet to be released to the public by authorities.

items.title: Plastic packaging price pain could push up costs for shoppers on everything from milk to make-up
items.summary: Companies that use plastic packaging are facing price pressures and supply disruptions, which they warn will likely mean higher costs for consumers. The cost of food-grade resin out of Singapore, which is used in milk bottles, has increased 110 per cent since the start of the Middle East war.

items.title: Australia lacks national strategy to regulate AI spread in workplace, report states
items.summary: Australia risks repeating the mistakes it made with social media unless it moves quickly to regulate the spread of artificial intelligence in the workplace, according to a new report.

items.title: Hundreds of child abuse files found on devices belonging to police killer Dezi Freeman
items.summary: Police killer Dezi Freeman was on the verge of having his secret stash of child abuse material discovered when he gunned down two officers outside his makeshift home.

items.title: First Nations film Wolfram writes hope into heavy history
items.summary: Convinced to return to the story of the 2018 outback drama Sweet Country, Kaytetye filmmaker Warwick Thornton fell in love with the possibilities.

items.title: Worried about a neighbour's dog? Here's what you can do
items.summary: An unhappy dog doesn't necessarily mean criminal neglect, even if the level of care is below what you would give your own pet.

items.title: Travel quiz, April 29: Grab your ticket and a map, we're off on a quiz adventure
items.summary: The Galápagos Islands are part of which South American country? The Elgin Marbles were taken from which ancient site?

items.title: Super Netball Round-Up: Netball's powerful all-female statement on Anzac Day
items.summary: They both struggle for recognition in male-dominated fields, so Super Netball chose to host an all-female Anzac Day ceremony to commemorate and give visibility to female veterans.

items.title: WA government plans to build 500 homes despite 'critical worker shortage'
items.summary: The West Australian government has announced it plans to build 500 homes for public servants in regional areas over five years despite a "critical worker shortage".

items.title: Big tech hits back at Labor government's Media Bargaining Incentive plan
items.summary: US tech giant Meta has condemned Labor's plan to tax large digital platforms that fail to pay for using Australian journalism as "government-mandated transfer of wealth".

items.title: CCTV footage shows the moment underworld figure Gavin Preston was shot dead
items.summary: The Victorian Supreme Court has released footage of the moment underworld figure Gavin Preston was shot dead in Melbourne.

items.summary: Latest trusted news from the world of finance and business.

items.title: Perth man David Moussa pleads not guilty to stabbing father to death on grounds of insanity
items.summary: David Moussa, 32, pleads not guilty on grounds of insanity to murdering his father in the Perth suburb of Clarkson.

items.title: NuGrow to fight charges of nuisance odours from Ipswich facility
items.summary: Waste recycling company NuGrow will fight 11 environmental nuisance charges relating to its Swanbank food recycling plant in a two-week trial later this year.

items.title: Erotic fiction author Lauren Mastrosa spared jail over child abuse material

items.summary: Police are investigating whether recent arson attacks across 15 Melbourne hospitality venues are linked to an organised crime syndicate.

items.title: Rockhampton's Plaza Hotel set to go under the hammer, but no inspections allowed
items.summary: Rockhampton's Plaza Hotel is set to go under the hammer after closing down more than a decade ago.

items.title: Farmers and authorities on alert as Australian plague locust migrates to South Australia
items.summary: Multiple locust swarms in South Australia's Riverland region have put farmers and authorities on alert, prompting the South Australian Plague Locust Commissioner to send surveying teams to the region.

items.title: Mother 'always thinks' about whether birth delay at regional hospital led to son's ongoing health issues
items.summary: A regional South Australian mother who faced a range of stressful diversions during her son's birth says she will always wonder whether his serious and ongoing health concerns were caused by delays in receiving care.

items.title: Alex Johnston and Jarome Luai set to join PNG Chiefs in major get for NRL expansion team
items.summary: Record-setting South Sydney try-scoring winger Alex Johnston looks set to become the first player to sign for the PNG Chiefs ahead of their 2028 NRL entry.

items.title: Donald Trump names former Republican congressman as new US ambassador to Australia
items.summary: The Trump administration has nominated former congressman David Brat as ambassador to Australia.

items.title: Health insurers accused of 'aggressive' and 'almost bullying' tactics in private hospital dealings
items.summary: A former Bupa executive accuses health insurers of using "almost bullying" tactics in their negotiations with private hospitals, while the Australian Private Hospitals Association says insurers have become "cowboys" in need of regulation.

items.title: Why NSW hasn’t met a problem Tiana Penitani Gray couldn’t solve
items.summary: Tiana Penitani Gray has never played second row before, but as the Origin stalwart prepares to play her third position in as many series such a bold change is nothing new.

items.title: Millie Elliott returns after having a baby to line up for NSW in State of Origin Game I
items.summary: Millie Elliott could have taken an easy route back into competitive action after having a baby. Instead, she is throwing herself straight back into the State of Origin cauldron on Thursday night.

items.title: Nedra Talley Ross, the last surviving member of the 1960s pop trio The Ronettes, dies aged 80
items.summary: Nedra Talley Ross, the last surviving member of the 1960s pop band the Ronettes, who sang the enduring hits Be My Baby, Baby I Love You, and Walking in the Rain alongside her cousins, has died aged 80.

items.title: Coalition points to next-generation stealth bombers as potential AUKUS stopgap
items.summary: The Coalition says Australia should consider buying next-generation stealth bombers to help bridge the gap until the AUKUS submarines arrive.

items.title: Single-use plastic soy sauce fish are an eco-nightmare. A local designer has a solution
items.summary: Shoyu-tai, the single-use plastic soy sauce fish that accompany takeaway sushi, are an eco-nightmare — but a local designer has a solution.

items.title: Dolphin spotted in Melbourne's Yarra River probably hunting prey, researcher says
items.summary: Researchers say the dolphin appears healthy and is most likely chasing prey in the Melbourne waterway.

items.title: What I've learnt about living on other mobs' Country — respectfully
items.summary: To better understand what respect looks like in practice, I spoke with other First Nations people about how they approach working and living on other mobs' Countries.

items.title: Incat electric ferry waiting in Hobart as heavy-lift transporter is stuck in Strait of Hormuz
items.summary: The world's largest electric ferry is sitting idle in Tasmania after construction was completed because the heavy-lift ship that was booked to take it to South America is stuck in the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: Former Australian federal commissioner Tom Rogers to lead independent review into SA election
items.summary: Former Australian electoral commissioner Tom Rogers is appointed to lead a "sweeping independent review" of the management and oversight of last month's SA state election and First Nations Voice election.

items.title: Suspect in Washington dinner shooting charged with trying to assassinate Donald Trump
items.summary: The man accused of opening fire at a Washington dinner attended by Donald Trump has been charged with attempting to assassinate the US president and could face life in prison if convicted.

items.title: Rebel Wilson describes herself as 'champion of women' as actor gives evidence in defamation trial
items.summary: The Hollywood star is being sued for defamation in the Federal Court by Charlotte MacInnes, who plays the lead actor in Ms Wilson's debut directorial film The Deb.

items.title: Broken PBS system behind failure to list Mounjaro for type 2 diabetics, according to Medicines Australia
items.summary: The "broken" process behind listing medication on the PBS contributes to hundreds of thousands of Australians with diabetes missing out on subsidised lifesaving medication, according to the peak industry body.

items.title: Donald Trump calls for broadcaster to fire Jimmy Kimmel
items.summary: Donald Trump says Jimmy Kimmel should be fired for a monologue he delivered days before the third alleged attempt to assassinate the US president.

items.title: Iran blames US for failed peace talks during visit to Russia — as it happened
items.summary: Iran's foreign minister says the US's "excessive demands" were the cause of failed peace talks. Meanwhile, Donald Trump has discussed the latest Iranian proposal. Look back on our live blog.

items.title: Indigenous players defend St Kilda coach Ross Lyon after report reveals comment during training session
items.summary: Several indigenous players who have played under Saints coach Ross Lyon come to his defence after comments he made during a training session led to a meeting where Lyon offered to stand down.

items.title: NFL kicker Younghoe Koo's comical error revealed a brain tumour in fan Mark Toothaker
items.summary: Mark Toothaker laughed so hard at Younghoe Koo's botched NFL kick that he had a seizure. It may have saved his life.

items.title: CCTV to be installed on Gold Coast waterways to crack down on illegal boaties
items.summary: The Gold Coast Waterways Authority says the initiative comes after more than 400 unregistered and unseaworthy vessels were removed from the city's waterways over the past five years.

items.title: Wyndham councillors pass vote of no confidence in mayor Preet Singh over character reference scandal
items.summary: Wyndham councillors in Melbourne's south-west unanimously pass a vote of no confidence in Mayor Preet Singh over a character reference he provided to a convicted child sex offender.

items.title: Push to make voluntary assisted dying available through telehealth
items.summary: Despite voluntary assisted dying being legal in Victoria, many regional patients are not getting the same end-of-life care.

items.title: New York mayor announces free FIFA World Cup fan zones as costs continue to escalate
items.summary: New York City will host free events for soccer fans who can't afford the hefty ticket prices for the FIFA World Cup, Mayor Zohran Mamdani says.

items.title: Doctors concerned about $3.2b new Women’s and Children’s Hospital design
items.summary: Doctors say parts of the proposed design for Adelaide's $3.2 billion new Women's and Children's Hospital raise "significant concerns", including the location of the women's assessment service.

items.summary: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

items.title: Farmer rescues kangaroo from mud in dry dam as drought conditions hit western NSW
items.summary: Photos show a kangaroo moments before being rescued from a dam in Central West NSW, after it became bogged in mud while searching for water in an increasingly barren landscape.

items.title: Josh Hazlewood delivers IPL masterclass as Royal Challengers Bengaluru smash Delhi Capitals
items.summary: Australian pace maestro Josh Hazlewood delivers his best performance of the IPL season, taking four wickets to spearhead another RCB win.

items.title: Scenic Rim community divided over proposed Cleanaway waste-to-energy incinerator
items.summary: A proposed waste-to-energy incinerator near Beaudesert divides the Scenic Rim community. Supporters say it could ease Queensland's landfill crisis, while opponents fear health and environmental risks.

items.title: Australian rugby may benefit from copying Crusaders' lateral thinking with Leicester Fainga'anuku
items.summary: Thinking outside the square has previously served Australian rugby well, which is why there are lessons it can take away from at least one of New Zealand's teams in Super Rugby Pacific.

items.title: Penny Wong will have to navigate China, Japan and South Korea tensions
items.summary: When Foreign Minister Penny Wong travels to Japan, China and South Korea this week, she will move through a region where relationships point in different directions at the same time.

items.summary: There are some key moments coming up for the Australian economy and they could make a big difference to your bank account. Emily Stewart — aka Sensible Emily — is here to help you understand what to look out for.

items.title: ABC Places quiz: Where in the world are we?
items.summary: Today's ABC Places quiz features photos from 10 locations around the world. Your challenge is to guess where you are.
